tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypercreatininemia, proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, with cloudy, brown, ammoniacal urine but no leukocytes or nitrites. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low-normal, suggesting no chronic hyperglycemia. Findings may indicate possible renal dysfunction (tubular/glomerular), and the urine abnormalities could suggest acute kidney injury or nephrotic syndrome. Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical findings.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: KfH-Nierenzentrum am Klinikum Freising
